value.found_quote: You can test the MCP server using the Responses API directly with a request like this one... "tools": [ { "type": "mcp", "server_label": "cats", "server_url": "https://777ff573-9947-4b9c-8982-658fa40c7d09-00-3le96u7wsymx.janeway.replit.dev/sse/", "allowed_tools": [ "search", "fetch" ], "require_approval": "never" } ]
value.rationale: The page explicitly demonstrates that remote MCP servers can be declared as tools in the Responses API (via the `tools` array with `"type": "mcp"`), allowing models to call them programmatically. The curl example against `https://api.openai.com/v1/responses` shows the MCP server configured as a built-in tool type in the Responses API, directly substantiating the claim. The page treats this as a current, generally available capability with no preview or beta caveats attached.
